items.headline: S&P 500's Nine-Week Rally Ends Amid Sharp Selloff; Nasdaq and S&P 500 Record Worst Day of the Year
items.summary: The broader market experienced a significant downturn today, with the S&P 500 ending its nine-week rally and both the Nasdaq and S&P 500 recording their worst single-day performance of the year. A stronger-than-expected May employment report released on Friday triggered a risk-off sentiment, leading to a sharp increase in bond yields and a broad selloff in equities. Investors rotated out of high-beta, momentum, and growth stocks, particularly those in the AI and semiconductor sectors, and moved into more defensive and value-oriented assets, as well as energy.

items.headline: Solidion Technology Inc. Leads Monthly Gains
items.summary: Solidion Technology, Inc. ($STI) was a top performer for the month, showing a remarkable gain of 624.54% with a price of $35.72, signaling strong momentum early in June.

items.headline: Laser Photonics Corporation Sees Significant Monthly Jump
items.summary: Laser Photonics Corporation ($LASE) also experienced a substantial monthly increase of 234.23%, trading at $3.1.

items.headline: Julong Holding Limited Continues Strong Monthly Performance
items.summary: Julong Holding Limited ($JLHL) posted a monthly gain of 137.04% to reach $44.8, reflecting sustained buying interest.

items.headline: Above Food Ingredients Inc. Plummets After NASDAQ Letter
items.summary: Above Food Ingredients Inc. ($ABVE) suffered an 80.57% decline after receiving a NASDAQ Determination Letter under listing rules 5250(c)(1) and 5250(c)(2).

items.headline: Jianzhi Education Technology Group Company Limited Down on Direct Offering
items.summary: Jianzhi Education Technology Group Company Limited ($JZ) fell by 77.42% following the announcement of a $5 Million Registered Direct Offering.

items.headline: Quantum Computing Stocks Tumble Amid Profit-Taking
items.summary: Rigetti Computing, Inc. ($RGTI) dropped 14.55% as investors took profits in high-flying quantum computing names.

items.headline: Strong May Jobs Report Fuels Rate Hike Concerns and Market Selloff
items.summary: The U.S. labor market showed robust growth in May, with 172,000 new nonfarm payroll jobs reported, significantly exceeding consensus expectations and keeping unemployment at 4.3%. This strong economic data, however, triggered a 'good news is bad news' scenario for the stock market, as it amplified concerns about potential Federal Reserve rate hikes to tame a hot economy. The 10-year Treasury yield jumped to 4.54%, and traders are now pricing in a 57% chance of a Fed rate hike this year.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Sees Investors Exit Tech and Growth for Value and Defensives
items.summary: Today's market witnessed a clear rotation away from high-beta, momentum, and growth stocks, particularly in the technology and AI sectors, towards more stable value, defensive, and energy plays. While Industrials, Technology, and Healthcare showed strong average monthly gains in June, the immediate market reaction saw consumer staples rise 1.6%, and financials and healthcare finding buyers, contrasting with sharp declines in chip stocks and quantum computing companies.

items.headline: Mixed Earnings Season with Focus on Upcoming Reports and AI Impact
items.summary: The first quarter 2026 earnings season saw fewer companies outperforming forecasts, with an increase in earnings misses, indicating a challenging environment. Looking ahead, major earnings reports from Oracle ($ORCL) on June 10 and Adobe ($ADBE) on June 11 are highly anticipated. Zscaler ($ZS) experienced a significant sell-off after providing a disappointing outlook for its 2027 annualized recurring revenue growth, highlighting investor sensitivity to guidance.

items.headline: Central Banks Under Scrutiny with ECB Rate Hike Expected
items.summary: Beyond the Federal Reserve's next FOMC meeting on June 16-17, the European Central Bank (ECB) is widely expected to implement a 25 basis-point rate hike this week. The ongoing global environment, marked by geopolitical tensions and surging energy costs, continues to influence central bank decisions as they navigate between economic growth and inflation control.
